world.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel breaking April ceasefire

main.text: Iran fires missiles towards Israel for first time since April ceasefire
main.note: Direct report from established news outlet with live link.

perspectives.text: They’re out here boycotting Chinese restaurants and Asian nail salons over one incident that the owners had absolutely nothing to do with. Imagine if white people or any other race organized a boycott against black owned businesses after a crime made the news. It would be called a hate crime and lead every single news channel in the country.
perspectives.note: Sharp cultural critique tying into broader conflict fallout.

perspectives.text: If Iran's critical infrastructure is attacked, there will be no critical infrastructure left in occupied Palestine, nor in the complicit Arab family dictatorships in the Persian Gulf. That will mean the end of the global economy as we know it.
perspectives.note: Direct analysis from Iranian academic on escalation risks.

world.headline: Luka Modrić equals Messi on 198 international caps ahead of World Cup

main.text: Luka Modrić scored his 29th goal for Croatia in their final World Cup warm-up match against Slovenia. He equals Leo Messi on 198 international caps—both are on the verge of joining Cristiano Ronaldo and Kuwait's Bader Al-Mutawa in playing at least 200 games for their country
main.note: Verified sports stats from official football account.

perspectives.text: "He's in a sweet spot and in top shape." Thomas Tuchel on Jude Bellingham's fitness with England's World Cup opener against Croatia 10 days away
perspectives.note: Coaching insight on key player preparation.

nation.headline: FBI and federal prosecutors investigate multi-level election fraud in California

main.text: I’ve been calling this out for years, and they called us crazy. But finally, the curtain is being pulled back. With federal prosecutors and the FBI now on the ground investigating multi-level election fraud in California, the "nothing to see here" excuse is officially dead.
main.note: Candidate statement on active federal probe.

perspectives.text: They want us to believe Nithya Raman surged post Election Day due to her voting base of… Skid Row Crackheads. Think about that.
perspectives.note: Skeptical take on sudden ballot shifts.

nation.headline: Reform UK insiders blame Restore Britain for Makerfield by-election loss

main.text: 🚨NEW: Reform UK insiders believe that Restore Britain has cost them the Makerfield by-election. They have also said: "We have to learn and understand why people are choosing them over us"
main.note: Insider political reporting on UK by-election.

perspectives.text: From a follower on a thread in FB about Makerfield I'm angry June! I live in the Makerfield constituency it's only 2 years since we had the election, we neither wanted of asked for this bloody stupid by-election

nation.headline: US Attorney confirms limits on federal authority over state election rules

main.text: Congress has not provided us with that authority. I’m limited to enforcing federal law as currently written. Congress may change any of these rules at its will.

business.headline: US stock futures fall amid Iran-Israel tensions; oil surges

main.text: BREAKING: US stock market futures fall as markets reopen for the first time since Israel's attack on Beirut and Iran's retaliation: 1. S&P 500: -0.3% 2. Nasdaq 100: -0.3% 3. Dow Jones: -0.3% 4. WTI Crude: +4.0% 5. Brent: +3.5%
main.note: Real-time market data from finance commentary account.

business.headline: Jay Electronica no-shows Las Vegas concert prompting promoter backlash

main.text: Jay Electronica had show in Las Vegas last night and he didn't show up "F*ck Jay Electronica man" -Concert promoter

elon.text: Maybe largest in this sector of the galaxy
Indeed. It’s not that good ideas aren’t important, they’re just overrated relative to implementation. The IDEA of going to the Moon is EASY, but GOING to the Moon is HARD.
Why is it only pushed in the West?
The reason ID is banned in California (and New York) elections is to enable large-scale fraud. When you combine no ID and mail-in voting, fraud is de facto legalized.
Getting France on our side was worth it

sports.headline: Denny Hamlin earns 63rd Cup Series win tying Kyle Busch

main.text: With 63 Cup Series wins, Denny Hamlin ties Kyle Busch at 9th on the all-time wins list. Paying tribute to Rowdy.

free.headline: Andreessen on the "greengrocer lie" — why we chant slogans we know are false
free.text: Marc Andreessen on preference falsification: the 1985 Prague greengrocer parable, back again.
free.note: Andreessen unpacking Timur Kuran's "Private Truths, Public Lies" + Václav Havel's greengrocer parable. No speculation — maps directly onto modern forced public compliance.
